action: Build a balanced add-liquidity intent, pin the pool and active-bin snapshot, simulate exact calldata, and review net placement, protected amounts, fees, residuals, targets, and gas before submission.
actor: Connected liquidity provider with both required assets

expectedAssertions.statement: The review binds pool identity, active ID, distribution, recipient, refund target, deadline, and exact calldata inputs.
Simulation and deterministic fee math disclose net X and Y placed, protected minima, composition fee and protocol share, and expected leftovers by asset.
Estimated gas is buffered and labeled non-guaranteed, separately from composition fee, slippage, and residual assets.
Submission is disabled when simulation reverts or pinned state becomes stale.

negativePaths.trigger: Exact calldata simulation reverts for the reviewed balanced distribution.
Active ID or pool state changes after review is created.

prerequisites: A canonical attested pool and funded owner are available.
Both asset allowances can be established.
Exact add-liquidity simulation results are available.
recovery: Refresh live pool, balance, allowance, fee, and gas state.
Re-simulate exact calldata and require a new immutable review.
title: Pin complete balanced liquidity review

action: Edit lower and upper bounds through bin IDs, deltas, quote-per-base prices, inverse prices, and range selection while observing every synchronized representation.
actor: Liquidity provider configuring a position range

expectedAssertions.statement: Changing any bound updates bin ID, active-bin delta, normalized quote-per-base price, inverse price, and range selection consistently.
Unequal token decimals are normalized before price-to-bin and bin-to-price conversion.
Round trips use the representable bin price and disclose rounding rather than presenting an unattainable exact price.
Keyboard and direct numeric edits preserve ordered, reachable controls without hidden bound changes.

negativePaths.trigger: Token X and token Y use unequal decimals and inverse display is enabled.
A keyboard edit moves one bound across the other bound.

prerequisites: A supported pool with known active ID and token decimals is selected.
recovery: Restore the last valid ordered bounds when an edit is invalid.
Review the synchronized representable prices before continuing.
title: Synchronize LP bin and price range controls

action: Apply narrow and wide concentration presets, edit the resulting bounds, and move the active bin inside and outside the selected range.

expectedAssertions.statement: Narrow and wide presets create deterministic editable ranges within the product bin limit.
Preset labeling explains concentration and out-of-range exposure without promising returns.
Manual edits detach or update the preset state without silently restoring preset bounds.
Active-bin movement refreshes in-range and out-of-range risk copy and invalidates a stale review.

negativePaths.trigger: The active bin moves beyond a previously selected narrow range.
The user manually expands a narrow preset after applying it.

prerequisites: A pool with a known active bin is selected.
Narrow and wide presets are available.
recovery: Refresh the active bin and recompute the selected range state.
Review the edited bounds and current risk disclosure before submission.
title: Apply editable concentration presets with truthful risk

action: Enter boundary and invalid LP ranges including one, sixty-nine, and seventy bins, inverted bounds, unrepresentable prices, overflow values, and decimal-normalization errors.
actor: Liquidity provider entering custom range bounds

expectedAssertions.statement: Ranges containing one through sixty-nine bins are accepted when otherwise representable and ordered.
A seventy-bin range is rejected before simulation or wallet submission.
Inverted, non-finite, overflowed, unrepresentable, or decimal-invalid bounds are rejected with a field-specific explanation.
Invalid edits cannot mutate the last valid distribution or transaction review.

negativePaths.trigger: The inclusive selected range expands from sixty-nine to seventy bins.
A bound is inverted, outside representable price space, overflowed, or invalid after decimal normalization.

prerequisites: Custom bin and price range inputs are available.
Simulation and wallet calls can be observed.
recovery: Return to the last valid ordered representable bounds.
Recompute the distribution and create a fresh review.
title: Enforce representable one-to-sixty-nine-bin ranges

action: Configure active-bin-imbalanced and one-sided liquidity adds, simulate exact calldata, and inspect estimated composition fee and protocol share before submission.
actor: Liquidity provider adding near the active bin

expectedAssertions.statement: The review distinguishes composition fee and protocol share from gas, price movement, slippage, and unplaced assets.
Estimated fees use the pinned active-bin state and exact proposed distribution.
Balanced, imbalanced, and one-sided fixtures agree with SDK and contract fee math.
Fee drift or active-bin movement invalidates the review instead of preserving a stale estimate.

negativePaths.trigger: Liquidity crosses the active bin with an imbalanced composition that incurs a composition fee.
The active bin or fee parameters change after simulation.

prerequisites: A pool and distribution capable of incurring composition fees are available.
SDK fee calculations and exact simulation results can be compared.
recovery: Re-simulate and require the provider to review the updated fee estimate.
title: Disclose composition fee and protocol share

action: Configure a range around the active bin, inspect concentration and one-sided direction, then move the active bin or edit bounds before submitting.

expectedAssertions.statement: The form explains whether the current range is in range, below, or above the active bin and which asset side is expected.
Concentration risk, one-sided direction, and out-of-range behavior are disclosed alongside synchronized bounds.
Active-bin movement updates range classification, fee estimate, net placement, and residual expectations.
Any active-state or bound change invalidates the pinned review and prevents stale submission.

negativePaths.trigger: The active bin moves from inside the selected range to outside it after review.
The provider edits a reviewed range from wide to highly concentrated.

prerequisites: A selected pool exposes current active-bin state.
The provider can edit bounds and trigger active-bin movement.
recovery: Refresh live active-bin state and recompute all synchronized range values.
Re-simulate the updated distribution and require a new risk review.
title: Refresh LP range risk when active state changes

action: Simulate an add expected to place less than the supplied amounts and inspect protected minima, leftovers, recipient, refund target, and asset-specific residual handling.
actor: Connected liquidity provider funding an add transaction

expectedAssertions.statement: The immutable review names the position recipient and refund target and binds both to calldata.
Expected net X and Y placed, protected minimum amounts, and leftover X and Y are shown separately.
Residual assets are identified by actual token identity and are not hidden as slippage, fees, or gas.
A changed recipient, refund target, minimum, or residual estimate invalidates the prior review.

negativePaths.trigger: Simulation returns nonzero unplaced X or Y for the proposed distribution.
The recipient or refund target changes after simulation.

prerequisites: An add fixture produces nonzero residual assets.
Exact simulation returns placed and leftover amounts.
recovery: Restore or explicitly choose the intended recipient and refund target.
Re-simulate exact calldata and review updated minima and residual assets.
title: Bind LP minima, residual assets, and refund targets

action: Submit an add-liquidity transaction, reload while it is pending, and inspect persistence and canonical reconciliation of the reviewed add intent.
actor: Connected liquidity provider with a submitted add intent

expectedAssertions.statement: The journal persists owner, chain, deployment, pool, recipient, refund target, value, distribution, protected minima, settings fingerprint, nonce, and hash.
Reload resumes the same pending add intent without another wallet prompt or duplicate journal record.
Confirmation, replacement, timeout, revert, and orphan states remain attached to the same intent lineage.
A new form intent clears stale form-scoped banners but preserves owner-scoped transaction history.

negativePaths.trigger: The application reloads before the add transaction is mined.
The pending add is replaced, times out, reverts, or is orphaned.

prerequisites: A persistent owner-scoped transaction journal is enabled.
An add transaction can remain pending across reload.
recovery: Resume monitoring the persisted nonce and hash lineage.
Allow a fresh add only after the prior submission is canonically resolved or proven absent and a new review is completed.
title: Resume pending liquidity add from persistent journal

action: Complete an add-liquidity transaction and reconcile its canonical receipt, composition-fee event, wallet deltas, residual assets, and per-bin balances against the immutable review.
actor: Liquidity provider whose add transaction reached the required confirmations

expectedAssertions.statement: Receipt status, chain, contract, sender, nonce, transaction value, and decoded add parameters match the reviewed intent.
Actual composition fee and protocol share are derived from the canonical event and are distinguished from the pre-submit estimate.
Wallet X and Y deltas, returned residual assets, and recipient per-bin balances reconcile with the receipt.
A reverted, mismatched, orphaned, or noncanonical receipt never produces successful position state.

negativePaths.trigger: The observed receipt reverts or its sender, nonce, destination, value, or decoded parameters differ from the reviewed intent.
The receipt is orphaned or its fee event, residuals, wallet deltas, or per-bin balances do not reconcile.

prerequisites: A submitted add intent and immutable review exist.
Canonical receipt, logs, wallet balances, and per-bin balances are queryable.
recovery: Demote mismatched or orphaned records to reconciliation and refresh canonical chain state.
Present the specific receipt or balance discrepancy and do not finalize the position until reconciliation succeeds.
title: Reconcile actual liquidity add receipt and balances
